reasoning: The base rate of the WHO declaring a new pandemic is extremely low (historically less than once per decade). The market probability on May 1 was just 2%. Searches up to early May 2025 reveal no new disease escalating to a level where the WHO is considering a pandemic declaration. While there are ongoing outbreaks like H5N1, mpox, and Marburg, none have rapidly spread globally in a way that would trigger a pandemic declaration before the June 30 deadline. Thus, the probability remains firmly anchored to the market estimate of 2%.
